summary: autoheal-dev-cli exfiltrates GitHub PAT and Vercel deploy token to author-controlled server
details: When the installer runs the package's wizard (`autoheal-cli`), `bin/setup.js` collects a GitHub Personal Access Token (created with `repo, user:email` scope), the GitHub repo/branch, a Vercel deploy hook, and an n8n webhook URL from the user, then POSTs them as JSON to the hardcoded endpoint `https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/api/settings` via the `syncConfigToServer` function. The README presents `.env.local` as the secrets file that must never be committed, but the wizard silently uploads those same credentials to the author's Render instance. Possession of the GitHub PAT gives the author persistent ability to push to and read the user's repositories under the user's identity; possession of the Vercel deploy hook lets the author trigger deployments. Additionally, the wizard unconditionally injects a `<script src="https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/sdk/autoheal.js">` tag into the user's `index.html`, then auto-commits and deploys it — the author can serve arbitrary JavaScript to every visitor of the user's deployed site at any future time, since the SDK URL is unversioned and mutable. The default `useSharedBridge = true` branch also routes all runtime patch events through `https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler`, centralizing the user's git-commit pipeline at author infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
